etymology.description: semantic 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass; plant") + phonetic 枼(枼) (yè, "leaf") -> depiction of a leaf
etymology.interpretation: The character 葉(yè, "leaf") is formed by combining 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass; plant") as the semantic component, representing its association with plants, with 枼(枼) (yè, "leaf") as the phonetic component conveying the pronunciation. This results in the character meaning 'leaf,' representing the part of a plant.
etymology.simplification: In the simplified version 叶(葉), the semantic 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass; plant") is removed while retaining the phonetic 十(十), maintaining the connection to the sound but with fewer strokes.

examples.chinese: 秋天到了(秋天到了)，树上的叶子变黄了(樹上的葉子變黃了)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūtiān dàole, shù shàng de yèzi biàn huáng le.
examples.english: Autumn has arrived, and the leaves on the trees have turned yellow.

examples.chinese: 一片叶子飘落在地上(一片葉子飄落在地上)。
examples.pinyin: Yí piàn yèzi piāoluò zài dì shàng.
examples.english: A leaf drifted down to the ground.

examples.chinese: 风吹过时(風吹過時)，树叶沙沙作响(樹葉沙沙作響)。
examples.pinyin: Fēng chuīguò shí, shùyè shāshā zuòxiǎng.
examples.english: When the wind blows, the leaves rustle.
